name: Korean Braised Gochujang Chicken (Dak-bokkeum-tang)
why: A proper Korean one-pot braised chicken stew — the chicken cooks low and slow in a gochujang-rich sauce until it practically falls off the bone, with potatoes and carrots soaking up all that flavour. Perfect comfort food technique you haven't tried yet.
author: Henry
ingredients: 8 chicken pieces (4 drumsticks & 4 bone-in chicken thighs)
5 potatoes (about 800g), peeled and cut into large chunks
4 tbsp gochugaru chilli flakes, or to taste (use 1 tbsp sweet paprika for milder)
1 large carrot (about 200g), peeled and cut into large chunks
2 brown onions, peeled and cut into large chunks

steps: Place the chicken pieces and potato chunks in a large, deep sauté or braising pan.
Add the bay leaves, gochujang, gochugaru chilli flakes, sugar, soy sauce, mirin, honey, salt, and black pepper.
Toss everything together until the chicken and potatoes are evenly coated in the seasoning mixture.
Add 4 cups of water to the pan and place over high heat until just simmering.
Cover with a lid, reduce heat to medium, and simmer for 20 minutes. The chicken absorbs all the flavours and the potatoes begin to soften.
Remove the lid and add the carrot and onion chunks.
Cover again and simmer for another 15 minutes, or until the carrots and potatoes are just tender when pierced with a fork. The sauce should be reducing and thickening slightly.
Gently stir through the chopped garlic, sliced red chillies, and spring onion batons.
Cover and simmer for another 2 minutes — just enough to soften the spring onions without losing their bright colour.
Remove from heat and serve immediately with steamed rice.

pro_tips: Cut potatoes and carrots into generously large chunks — they hold their shape better during the long braise and give a satisfying bite.
Let the dish rest for 10-15 minutes after cooking before serving — the sauce thickens and the flavours deepen and meld together beautifully.
